race.name: La Diagonela
race.tagline: The Engadin's pro-tour classic — 55 kilometers at altitude where Ski Classics elites battle beneath the Bernina massif.

vitals.prize_purse: Ski Classics Pro Tour prize money
vitals.aid_stations: Multiple stations along the Engadin valley

climate.description: Mid-January at 1,800m altitude guarantees cold, dry conditions with excellent snow. The Engadin's famous sunshine often provides clear skies but temperatures well below freezing. Altitude affects performance significantly for unacclimatized racers.
challenges: High altitude (1,800m) significantly impacts aerobic capacity
Dry alpine air requires aggressive hydration strategy
Strong UV at altitude — eye and skin protection essential

course.primary: Point-to-point classic technique race from Pontresina to Zuoz through the Upper Engadin valley. The course follows the valley floor with moderate climbing and descending, offering stunning views of the Bernina massif, Piz Palü, and surrounding 4,000m peaks.
course.surface: Machine-groomed classic tracks on high-altitude valley floor
course.grooming: World-class grooming, Ski Classics Pro Tour standard
features: Ski Classics Pro Tour event — elite professional field
Classic technique only — tests traditional skill
Starts in Pontresina, gateway to the Bernina range

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Despite being founded only in 2014, Ski Classics Pro Tour status delivers maximum competitive depth. Perfect altitude, scenery, snow reliability, grooming, and organization scores. Smaller field and younger pedigree are the main limitations.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: Score >= 80 qualifies for TIER 1 on merit

history.summary: La Diagonela is the youngest race in this collection, founded in 2014 as a Ski Classics Pro Tour event. Despite its short history, it immediately established itself as a world-class event by leveraging the Engadin valley's existing infrastructure and the prestige of the Ski Classics series. The race complements the older Engadin Skimarathon (freestyle, March) by offering a classic-technique pro tour event in January.
notable_facts: Founded in 2014 — the newest Ski Classics Pro Tour event
Complements the Engadin Skimarathon (skate, March) in the same valley
Ski Classics Pro Tour attracts the world's best long-distance skiers
Course passes through the historic Romansch-speaking Upper Engadin
Pontresina start is at the foot of the Bernina range (4,049m)
Despite its youth, consistently delivers one of the season's most competitive fields
